name: The Archons
shortDescription: In Gnostic cosmology, the Archons are malevolent or ignorant beings who rule over the material world, preventing souls from ascending to the divine realm. They are often seen as emanations of a flawed or ignorant creator god.
description: The Archons are a central concept in Gnostic cosmology, representing the powers that control and maintain the material world, which is seen as imperfect and separate from the true, divine realm. These entities are typically depicted as being either malevolent or simply ignorant, lacking the true knowledge of the divine. They are often portrayed as emanations or offspring of a flawed creator god, frequently identified as Yaldabaoth or the Demiurge. Their role is to keep souls trapped in the material world, preventing them from achieving gnosis (spiritual knowledge) and ascending to the higher spiritual planes. The Archons are diverse in their nature and function, with some texts describing specific Archons with particular roles and attributes. Their names and characteristics vary across different Gnostic systems. The concept of the Archons reflects a dualistic worldview, where the material world is seen as a prison or illusion created by these imperfect beings, in contrast to the perfect and transcendent divine realm. Overcoming the influence of the Archons is a key goal in Gnostic belief, achieved through gnosis and spiritual liberation.

sources.title: Gnosis: The Nature and History of Gnosticism
sources.author: Kurt Rudolph
